V-M-080.scope: Phase-63 visible KrotPN logo verification for public landing, auth, protected user shell, admin shell, favicon/PWA adjacency, and Phase-64 email-template boundary.

module-checks.check-1: Audit /home/truffle/Загрузки/ДЛЯ КРОТА/logos and record selected logo source dimensions, output paths, and file sizes without committing oversized or unused assets.
module-checks.check-11: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-63.xml docs/modules/M-080.xml docs/verification/V-M-080.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml
module-checks.check-12: mygrace lint; git diff --check; protected diff guard for backend API, deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, VPN runtime, MTProto runtime, and secret surfaces

verification-matrix.target-1: Logo assets are selected from the operator-provided logo folder, copied into public asset paths with deterministic names, valid signatures, bounded dimensions, and no unnecessary large first-paint files.
verification-matrix.target-2: Public/auth surfaces show KrotPN brand identity compactly while preserving registration, login, password recovery, email verification, and tariff CTA clarity.
verification-matrix.target-3: User shell and user dashboard expose compact logo placement without crowding VPN, subscription, MTProto, config, referrals, settings, or mobile navigation workflows.
verification-matrix.target-4: Admin shell/login/dashboard expose compact logo placement without crowding admin navigation, users/devices lists, MTProto analytics, emergency controls, servers, plans, or analytics tables.
verification-matrix.target-5: Email template handling remains boundary-safe: existing body logo paths may be referenced, but Phase-63 must not redesign premium emails, change Resend payload semantics, or claim mailbox sender-avatar/BIMI control.
verification-matrix.target-6: Phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion proof shows no logo/text/icon overlap, no horizontal scroll, no hidden primary CTA, and no Matrix layer pointer interference.
verification-matrix.target-7: No backend API, auth/session, billing, VPN, MTProto, deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, certificate, DNS, or secret behavior changes are introduced.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: KrotPN logo is visible on public/auth entry points and compactly present in authenticated user/admin shells where it improves orientation.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Logo variants use size-appropriate PNG/ICO/public assets and do not increase first-paint or bundle budgets beyond documented limits.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: Mobile and tablet layouts keep logo clear-space without hiding forms, bottom navigation, admin actions, cards, tables, charts, or copy/download controls.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: Existing favicon/PWA/email-logo/BIMI documentation stays truthful and Phase-64 remains the owner of full premium email template redesign.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: A visible logo overlaps text, icons, input placeholders, nav items, badges, admin rows, charts, or CTA buttons.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: A large or wrong-format logo bloats public assets, dist output, or first-paint surfaces without an explicit budget note.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Implementation claims control over Gmail/Apple/Resend sender avatars instead of only body-logo/BIMI/static frontend surfaces.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Logo integration changes backend APIs, email token handling, Resend API payload semantics, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, DNS, VPN, MTProto, or secrets.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: Regression smokes lose Phase-55 art-direction balance, Phase-56 auth clarity, Phase-57/58 primary workflow reachability, Phase-61 responsive safety, or Phase-62 compactness.

telemetry-requirements.allowed: Asset path, dimensions, byte size, route name, viewport size, selector marker, screenshot path, build asset size, and pass/fail marker.
telemetry-requirements.forbidden: Passwords, access tokens, refresh tokens, reset/verify tokens, API keys, Resend/YooKassa secrets, WireGuard private keys, MTProto secrets, full proxy links, real emails, raw user IPs, payment identifiers, certificate private keys, and server credentials.
telemetry-requirements.redaction: Smoke output must not print email verification links, tokenized URLs, secret-bearing env values, or live operator infrastructure data.

phase-gates.gate-1: M-080 cannot pass until asset audit, source selection, visible placement checks, asset budget checks, and email/BIMI boundary checks pass.
phase-gates.gate-2: M-009, M-010, M-069, M-072, M-073, M-075, and M-076 regressions must stay green after visible logo integration.
phase-gates.gate-3: User/admin builds, Phase-63 smoke, Phase-55/56/57/58/61/62 regressions, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard must pass before Phase-63 can be marked done-local.
phase-gates.gate-4: Clean-deploy visual review should confirm real browser logo rendering on public/auth/user/admin surfaces, but local execution must not claim live mailbox avatar or BIMI provider proof.

V-M-080.autonomous-execution-safety: Safe for local autonomous execution if changes stay inside frontend/frontend-admin visible logo components/assets/CSS, existing email body-logo references, Phase-63 smoke, and MyGRACE docs. Stop execution if backend API changes, Resend payload changes, deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose changes, BIMI DNS changes, VPN/MTProto runtime changes, or secret-handling changes appear necessary.
